description: Exploration: FSS, DSS, route plotting, system scanning

entries.content: The Full Spectrum Scanner (FSS) resolves all bodies in a system from any point within the system and is the primary discovery tool. The Detailed Surface Scanner (DSS) must be used at close range to a specific body to map its surface, reveal geological and biological signals, and earn the mapping bonus on the exploration data.
entries._justification: Phase 1 seed entry. No changes needed.

entries.content: The maximum jump range of a ship at its current load is provided in the Loadout journal event as the field MaxJumpRange, measured in light-years. This value accounts for current fuel load, cargo mass, and fitted FSD. OmniCOVAS reads jump_range_ly directly from this field and does NOT recompute it from first principles. First-principles jump range calculation (using mass, FSD rating, boost) is reserved for the Exploration pillar (Pillar 3, Phase 5).
entries.source: Elite Dangerous journal documentation, Loadout event schema
entries._justification: Week 7. Documents the intentional architecture decision: Phase 2 reads Loadout.MaxJumpRange, not physics. Prevents accidental first-principles reimplementation in Pillar 1.

entries.topic: Typical Jump Range Values by Ship Class
entries.content: Jump ranges vary significantly by ship class and FSD engineering. Typical unengineered values: Sidewinder ~7-10 ly, Eagle ~14 ly, Cobra Mk III ~20-25 ly, Python ~8-15 ly (heavy cargo ship), Anaconda ~9-22 ly, Asp Explorer ~25-35 ly. Engineered FSDs (Increased Range, Long Range mod) can add 50-100% to base range. These are reference values only; actual range comes from Loadout.MaxJumpRange.
entries.source: community ship guides, EDDB ship data, in-game testing
entries._justification: Week 7. Reference for sanity-checking jump_range_ly values in tests and debugging. Medium confidence because these are approximate community values, not Frontier-published specs. Needs review against current game version.

entries.content: Flying through the jet cone of a neutron star applies a 4x FSD boost for the next hyperspace jump, allowing significantly longer single jumps than the ship's base MaxJumpRange. This boosted jump range is NOT reflected in Loadout.MaxJumpRange -- the boost is temporary and applied at jump time. Phase 2 does not model neutron boosts; this is Exploration pillar (Phase 5) territory.
entries._justification: Week 7. Documents a known gap in Phase 2 jump range tracking. Neutron boosts appear as anomalously long jumps in FSDJump event data -- Phase 5 will handle these.
